mission.title: argo-rollouts: blue-green stuck indefinitely during a scaling event with prePromotionAnalysis & previewReplicaCount
mission.description: blue-green stuck indefinitely during a scaling event with prePromotionAnalysis & previewReplicaCount. This issue affects 27+ users.

steps.title: Identify argo-rollouts troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your argo-rollouts deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n argo-rollouts -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo-rollouts
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo-rollouts -n argo-rollouts --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review argo-rollouts configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant argo-rollouts configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n argo-rollouts -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo-rollouts
kubectl get configmap -n argo-rollouts -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=argo-rollouts
```
Argo Rollouts v1.4.0

We have the following rollout:

```yaml
  strategy:
    blueGreen:
      activeService: guestbook-prod
      autoPromotionEnabled: true
      prePromotionAnalysis:
        templates:
        - templateName:

steps.title: Apply the fix for blue-green stuck indefinitely during a scaling event with…
steps.description:
During a scaling event, we will now only perform scaling against our ReplicaSets, and only reconcile:
* `status.availableReplicas`
* `status.HPAReplicas`

This fixes a bug where we would lose some information in status (because we did not perform the normal full logic of reconciliation).
```yaml
strategy:
    blueGreen:
      activeService: guestbook-prod
      autoPromotionEnabled: true
      prePromotionAnalysis:
        templates:
        - templateName: guestbook-prod-synthetics
      previewReplicaCount: 1
      previewService: guestbook-prod-preview
      scaleDownDelayRevisionLimit: 3
      scaleDownDelaySeconds: 21600
```

steps.title: Confirm blue-green stuck indefinitely during a scaling… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo-rollouts -n argo-rollouts --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n argo-rollouts --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
During a scaling event, we will now only perform scaling against our ReplicaSets, and only reconcile:
* `status.availableReplicas`
* `status.HPAReplicas`

This fixes a bug where we would lose some information in status (because we did not perform the normal full logic of reconciliation).

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with argo-rollouts installed or the issue environment reproducible.
